title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models represent an inference-time compute paradigm that can be applied to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The presentation maps RLM principles onto agent workflows and explores how Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse guide agents to recursively solve complex tasks with declarable outcomes.
writeup: This talk introduces Recursive Language Models as an inference-time compute paradigm and maps its principles onto coding agent workflows for higher performance and reliability. It matters because it frames recursion as a way for agents to handle complex, long-context tasks rather than relying on a single pass. The concrete takeaways: RLM principles are applied to agent workflows, and Claude Code Dynamic Workflows together with OpenProse guide agents to recursively solve complex tasks with declarable outcomes.
